replication_study.hypothesis: Participants assigned to the lottery frame will report a lower mean willingness to pay (WTP) for the two-outcome prospect (50% chance of $10, 50% chance of $20) than participants assigned to the gift certificate frame, operationalized as: mean(WTP | Cond = lottery) < mean(WTP | Cond = gift), among participants who correctly identified the lowest possible outcome (check == 3).

data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single-wave between-subjects framing experiment (gift vs lottery) aligned with Experiment 1 in Yang, Vosgerau, and Loewenstein (2013).
data_plan.sample_size: Total rows in file: ~281; final analysis N depends on non-missing WTP and comprehension filter (check == 3); will be computed at runtime and reported.
data_plan.access_details: Provided in repository; analysis assumes the file is available at /app/data/Data_Cleaned_22102020.csv within the container.

qualification.explanation: This dataset is an independent sample implementing the same core manipulation and measurement as the original Experiment 1 (gift certificate vs lottery framing; WTP in USD; comprehension checks). It is therefore qualified to test robustness of the focal framing effect on WTP in a new sample while maintaining high fidelity to the original task.
qualification.similarity_to_original: • Original framing and task details come from original_paper.pdf, Experiment 1 (Methods and Results; pp. 726–728): between-subjects framing (lottery vs uncertain gift certificate), two-outcome prospect with 50/50 $10/$20, WTP in dollars, comprehension/IMC checks, and main t-test showing lower WTP under lottery (t(99)=3.43, p<.001; mean gift=$9.52 vs lottery=$5.68). • The replication dataset’s codebook inside Script.R (comments) indicates equivalent constructs and checks: Lot_check and Gift_check where the correct response is 3 for “$10” as the lowest outcome, and coded WTP variables Lot_WTPc and Gift_WTPc (“We have removed Qualtrics meta-data ... and coded willingness to pay variables”). This shows high-fidelity operationalization. • initial_details.txt restates the focal claim and the exact contrast (lottery vs gift) consistent with Experiment 1 in the paper.
qualification.deviation_from_original: • Incentive compatibility: The original employed an incentive-compatible mechanism for a subset (five randomly selected participants; original_paper.pdf, Experiment 1 Methods). The replication dataset appears to be a standard Qualtrics self-report without demonstrated incentive-compatible purchases (Script.R comments: cleaned Qualtrics; no reservation-price implementation), which is a deviation. • Sample: Original Experiment 1 N=119 (post_registration.json; original_paper.pdf), while the replication dataset has ~281 raw rows, yielding a different sample size after exclusions. • Outlier behavior: The replication dataset contains occasional extreme WTP entries (e.g., 7000), necessitating either reliance on coded variables (Lot_WTPc/Gift_WTPc) and/or secondary capped analysis (<=20) as reflected in Script.R. • Frames: The replication focuses solely on the Experiment 1 contrast (lottery vs gift), not the broader frame set in later experiments (coin flip, raffle, gamble, voucher) described in original_paper.pdf.

data_plan.notes: Data encoding issues include a BOM-affected ID column (ï..Id) and presence of currency symbols or text in raw WTP fields; use the coded WTP variables (Lot_WTPc, Gift_WTPc). Comprehension check correctness is coded as 3 for the $10 lowest outcome; the focal analysis subsets to check == 3. Extreme WTP values exist; a robustness analysis capping WTP at <=20 is pre-specified in Script.R.

steps: Load the dataset from /app/data/Data_Cleaned_22102020.csv with NA handling for blanks and 'NA'.
Construct condition variable (Cond: lottery vs gift) using presence of Lot_check vs Gift_check; align the comprehension check with condition.
Construct WTP as coded numeric values from Lot_WTPc (lottery) and Gift_WTPc (gift); strip any stray symbols if needed.
Primary analysis subset: retain participants who correctly identified the lowest outcome (check == 3) and have non-missing WTP.
Compute descriptives (mean, sd, n) of WTP by condition.
Compute effect size (Cohen’s d with Hedges correction) and nonparametric Wilcoxon rank-sum test as robustness.
Secondary robustness: repeat the analysis on the subset with WTP <= 20 (as in Script.R).
Report Ns per group, mean differences, test statistics, p-values, and effect size.
planned_method.models: Welch two-sample t-test for mean difference in WTP between conditions; nonparametric Wilcoxon rank-sum as robustness. Effect size via Cohen’s d (Hedges correction).
planned_method.outcome_variable: WTP (USD), assembled from coded variables Lot_WTPc (lottery) and Gift_WTPc (gift).
planned_method.independent_variables: Cond (factor with two levels: lottery, gift).
planned_method.control_variables: None for the focal test. Optional robustness checks could include attention difficulty (Dif), gender (Gen), age (Age), but these are not required for the pre-registered focal contrast.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Mean difference in WTP between lottery and gift conditions; effect size Cohen’s d.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Welch two-sample t-test (two-tailed), with Wilcoxon rank-sum as robustness.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion for missing Cond/WTP; primary analysis further restricts to those with correct comprehension (check == 3).
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Single focal outcome and contrast; no correction applied. Secondary capped-WTP analysis is reported as robustness without alpha adjustment.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Alpha = 0.05 (two-tailed). Directional expectation is lottery < gift; we report two-tailed p-values with directionality and effect size.

files.Script.R: Original analysis script provided by the replication team. It loads libraries (psych, yarrr), sets a local Windows working directory, reads Data_Cleaned_22102020.csv from a local path, constructs Cond, WTP, and check, filters to correct comprehension (check == 3), runs describeBy, t.test(WTP~Cond), and cohen.d. It also runs a secondary analysis capped at WTP<=20. Issues: hard-coded local setwd path; file I/O not using /app/data; factor/levels assignment on a numeric vector may not function as intended; yarrr dependency is unnecessary; writes dataS.csv without an absolute path.
files.replication_analysis.R: New, container-ready script created for this replication. It reads the dataset from /app/data, robustly cleans coded WTP fields (strips currency symbols/text), builds Cond and aligned check, subsets to comprehension-correct cases, computes descriptives, Welch t-test, Cohen’s d (effsize), and Wilcoxon test, and writes the analysis subset to /app/data/dataS.csv. It fixes all path issues and avoids local setwd. Run with: Rscript /app/data/replication_analysis.R (after copying the script to /app/data or mounting the project).

codebase.notes: Dependencies: R packages psych and effsize. The original Script.R depends on yarrr for plotting/effects but analysis uses psych::describeBy and cohen.d (which is better provided by effsize here). All I/O should be directed to /app/data. No GPU or large memory needed.

instructions: Place replication_analysis.R in the container (e.g., mount project directory) or copy it into /app/data.
Outputs: console summaries (descriptives, Welch t-test, Cohen’s d, Wilcoxon), and /app/data/dataS.csv (subset used for analysis).
Optionally, re-run secondary robustness by filtering to WTP <= 20 inside the script block provided.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare direction (lottery < gift), the size of the mean difference and effect size (Cohen’s d), and statistical significance (t-statistic, p-value) to the original Experiment 1 benchmarks (original_paper.pdf; post_registration.json): mean gift ≈ $9.52 vs lottery ≈ $5.68 (diff ≈ $3.84), t ≈ 3.43, p < .001, partial eta-squared ≈ .11.
